author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Luke vii. 2, etc.; on the three dead persons whom the Lord raised.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Luke vii. 2, etc.; on the three dead persons whom the Lord raised.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The miracles of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ make indeed an impression on all who hear of, and believe them; but on different men in different ways.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For some amazed at His miracles done on the bodies of men, have no knowledge to discern the greater; whereas some admire the more ample fulfilment in the souls of men at the present time of those things which they hear of as having been wrought on their bodies.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord Himself saith, “For as the Father raiseth up the dead, and quickeneth them; even so the Son quickeneth whom He will.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not of course that the Son “quickeneth” some, the Father others; but the Father and the Son “quicken” the same; for the Father doeth all things by the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let no one then who is a Christian doubt, that even at the present time the dead are raised.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now all men have eyes, wherewith they can see the dead rise again in such sort, as the son of that widow rose, of whom we have just read out of the Gospel; but those eyes wherewith men see the dead in heart rise again, all men have not, save those who have risen already in heart themselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is a greater miracle to raise again one who is to live for ever, than to raise one who must die again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The widowed mother rejoiced at the raising again of that young man; of men raised again in spirit day by day does Mother Church rejoice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He indeed was dead in the body but they in soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: His visible death was bewailed visibly; their death invisible was neither enquired into nor perceived.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He sought them out who had known them to be dead; He Alone knew them to be dead, who was able to make them alive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if the Lord had not come to raise the dead, the Apostle would not have said, “Rise, thou that sleepest, and arise from the dead, and Christ shall give thee light.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You hear of one asleep in the words, “Rise, thou that sleepest;” but understand it of one dead when you hear, “And arise from the dead.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus they who are even dead in the body are often said to be asleep.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And certainly they all are but asleep, in respect of Him who is able to awaken them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in respect of thee, a dead man is dead indeed, seeing he will not awake, beat or prick or tear him as thou wilt.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But in respect of Christ, he was but asleep to whom it was said, “Arise,” and he arose forthwith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No one can as easily awaken another in bed, as Christ can in the tomb.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now we find that three dead persons were raised by the Lord “visibly,” thousands “invisibly.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nay, who knows even how many dead He raised visibly?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For all the things that He did are not written.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: John tells us this, “Many other things Jesus did, the which if they should be written, I suppose that the whole world could not contain the books.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then there were without doubt many others raised: but it is not without a meaning that the three are expressly recorded.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For our Lord Jesus Christ would that those things which He did on the body should be also spiritually understood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He did not merely do miracles for the miracles’ sake; but in order that the things which He did should inspire wonder in those who saw them, and convey truth to them who understand.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As he who sees letters in an excellently written manuscript, and knows not how to read, praises indeed the transcriber’s hand, and admires the beauty of the characters; but what those characters mean or signify he does not know; and by the sight of his eyes he is a praiser of the work, but in his mind has no comprehension of it; whereas another man both praises the work, and is capable of understanding it; such an one, I mean, who is not only able to see what is common to all, but who can read also; which he who has never learned cannot.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So they who saw Christ’s miracles, and understood not what they meant, and what they in a manner conveyed to those who had understanding, wondered only at the miracles themselves; whereas others both wondered at the miracles, and attained to the meaning of them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such ought we to be in the school of Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he who says that Christ only worked miracles, for the miracles’ sake, may say too that He was ignorant that it was not the time for fruit, when He sought figs upon the fig-tree.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it was not the time for that fruit, as the Evangelist testifies; and yet being hungry He sought for fruit upon the tree.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Did not Christ know, what any peasant knew?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What the dresser of the tree knew, did not the tree’s Creator know?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then when being hungry He sought fruit on the tree, He signified that He was hungry, and seeking after something else than this; and He found that tree without fruit, but full of leaves, and He cursed it, and it withered away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What had the tree done in not bearing fruit?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What fault of the tree was its fruitlessness?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No; but there are those who through their own will are not able to yield fruit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And barrenness is “their” fault, whose fruitfulness is their will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Jews then who had the words of the Law, and had not the deeds, were full of leaves, and bare no fruit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This have I said to persuade you, that our Lord Jesus Christ performed miracles with this view, that by those miracles He might signify something further, that besides that they were wonderful and great, and divine in themselves, we might learn also something from them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then see what He would have us learn in those three dead persons whom He raised.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He raised again the dead daughter of the ruler of the synagogue, for whom when she was sick petition was made to Him, that He would deliver her from her sickness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And as He is going, it is announced that she is dead; and as though He would now be only wearying Himself in vain, word was brought to her father, “Thy daughter is dead, why weariest thou the Master any further?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But He went on, and said to the father of the damsel, “Be not afraid, only believe.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He comes to the house, and finds the customary funeral obsequies already prepared, and He says to them, “Weep not, for the damsel is not dead, but sleepeth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He spake the truth; she was asleep; asleep, that is, in respect of Him, by whom she could be awakened.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So awakening her, He restored her alive to her parents.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So again He awakened that young man, the widow’s son, by whose case I have been now reminded to speak with you, Beloved, on this subject, as He Himself shall vouchsafe to give me power.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have just heard how he was awakened.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord “came nigh to the city; and behold there was a dead man being carried out” already beyond the gate.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Moved with compassion, for that the mother, a widow and bereaved of her only son, was weeping, He did what ye have heard, saying, “Young man, I say unto thee, Arise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He that was dead arose, and began to speak, and He restored him to his mother.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He awakened Lazarus likewise from the tomb.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in that case when the disciples with whom He was speaking knew that he was sick, He said (now “Jesus loved him”), “Our friend Lazarus sleepeth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They thinking of the sick man’s healthful sleep; say, “Lord, if he sleep he is well.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Then said Jesus,” speaking now more plainly, I tell you, “our friend Lazarus is dead.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in both He said the truth; “He is dead in respect of you, he is asleep in respect of Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These three kinds of dead persons, are three kinds of sinners whom even at this day Christ doth raise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that dead daughter of the ruler of the synagogue was within in the house, she had not yet been carried out from the secresy of its walls into public view.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There within was she raised, and restored alive to her parents.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the second was not now indeed in the house, but still not yet in the tomb, he had been carried out of the walls, but not committed to the ground.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who raised the dead maiden who was not yet carried out, raised this dead man who was now carried out, but not yet buried.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There remained a third case, that He should raise one who was also buried; and this He did in Lazarus.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There are then those who have sin inwardly in the heart, but have it not yet in overt act.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A man, for instance, is disturbed by any lust.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Lord Himself saith, “Whosoever looketh on a woman to lust after her, hath committed adultery with her already in his heart.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He has not yet in body approached her, but in heart he has consented; he has one dead within, he has not yet carried him out.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And as it often happens, as we know, as men daily experience in themselves, when they hear the word of God, as it were the Lord saying, “Arise;” the consent unto sin is condemned, they breathe again unto saving health and righteousness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The dead man in the house arises, the heart revives in the secret of the thoughts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This resurrection of a dead soul takes place within, in the retirement of the conscience, as it were within the walls of the house.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Others after consent proceed to overt act, carrying out the dead as it were, that that which was concealed in secret, may appear in public.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Are these now, who have advanced to the outward act, past hope?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Was it not said to the young man in the Gospel also, “I say unto thee, Arise”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Was he not also restored to his mother?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then he too who has committed the open act, if haply admonished and aroused by the word of truth, he rise again at the Voice of Christ, is restored alive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Go so far he could, perish for ever he could not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But they who by doing what is evil, involve themselves even in evil habit, so that this very habit of evil suffers them not to see that it is evil, become defenders of their evil deeds; are angry when they are found fault with; to such a degree, that the men of Sodom of old said to the righteous man who reproved their abominable design, “Thou art come to sojourn, not to give laws.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So powerful in that place was the habit of abominable filthiness, that profligacy now passed for righteousness, and the hinderer of it was found fault with rather than the doer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such as these pressed down by a malignant habit, are as it were buried.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea, what shall I say, Brethren?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In such sort buried, as was said of Lazarus, “By this time he stinketh.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That heap placed upon the grave, is this stubborn force of habit, whereby the soul is pressed down, and is not suffered either to rise, or breathe again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now it was said, “He hath been dead four days.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So in truth the soul arrives at that habit, of which I am speaking by a kind of four-fold progress.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there is first the provocation as it were of pleasure in the heart, secondly consent, thirdly the overt act, fourthly the habit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there are those who so entirely throw off things unlawful from their thoughts, as not even to feel any pleasure in them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There are those who do feel the pleasure, and do not consent to them; death is not yet perfected, but in a certain sort begun.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To the feeling of pleasure is added consent; now at once is that condemnation incurred.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: After the consent, progress is made unto the open act; the act changes into a habit; and a sort of desperate condition is produced, so as that it may be said, “He hath been dead four days, by this time he stinketh.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, the Lord came, to whom of course all things were easy; yet He found in that case as it were a kind of difficulty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He “groaned” in the spirit, He showed that there is need of much and loud remonstrance to raise up those who have grown hard by habit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet at the voice of the Lord’s cry, the bands of necessity were burst asunder.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The powers of hell trembled, and Lazarus is restored alive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Lord delivers even from evil habits those who “have been dead four days;” for this man in the Gospel, “who had been dead four days,” was asleep only in respect of Christ whose will it was to raise him again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what said He?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Observe the manner of his arising again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He came forth from the tomb alive, but he could not walk.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Lord said to the disciples; “Loose him, and let him go.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He” raised him from death, “they” loosed him from his bonds.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Observe how there is something which appertaineth to the special Majesty of God who raiseth up.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A man involved in an evil habit is rebuked by the word of truth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How many are rebuked, and give no ear!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who is it then who deals within with him who does give ear?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who breathes life into him within?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who is it who drives away the unseen death, gives the life unseen?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: After rebukes, after remonstrances, are not men left alone to their own thoughts, do they not begin to turn over in their minds how evil a life they are living, with how very bad a habit they are weighed down?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then displeased with themselves, they determine to change their life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such have risen again; they to whom what they have been is displeasing have revived: but though reviving, they are not able to walk.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These are the bands of their guilt.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Need then there is, that whoso has returned to life should be loosed, and let go.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This office hath He given to the disciples to whom He said, “Whatsoever ye shall bind on earth, shall be bound in heaven also.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then, dearly Beloved, in such wise hear these things, that they who are alive may live; they who are dead may live again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whether it be that as yet the sin has been conceived in the heart, and not come forth into open act; let the thought be repented of, and corrected, let the dead within the house of conscience arise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or whether he has actually committed what he thought of; let not even thus his case be despaired of.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The dead within has not arisen, let him arise when “he is carried out.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him repent him of his deed, let him at once return to life; let him not go to the depth of the grave, let him not receive the load of habit upon him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But peradventure I am now speaking to one who is already pressed down by this hard stone of his own habit, who is already laden with the weight of custom, who “has been in the grave four days already, and who stinketh.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet let not even him despair; he is dead in the depth below, but Christ is exalted on high.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He knows how by His cry to burst asunder the burdens of earth, He knows how to restore life within by Himself, and to deliver him to the disciples to be loosed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let even such as these repent.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when Lazarus had been raised again after the four days, no foul smell remained in him when he was alive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then let them who are alive, still live; and let them who are dead, whosoever they be, in which kind soever of these three deaths they find themselves, see to it that they rise again at once with all speed.
